element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Defend village from Yellow Turban rebels with starter units
Defeat enemy generals in random battles, pay bribes (money/horses) to recruit
Manage 7-member active party (5 combat, 1 reserve, 1 tactician)
Leverage tactician support abilities for all party members
Liu Bei assumes Xu Province governorship, begins unification campaign
Encounter Kingdom of Sun Wu and Kingdom of Cao Wei in invasions
Choose alternate narrative paths with minimal story impact
Manage party slots (70 max), recruit/eject generals strategically
Many generals do not scale with experience; optimal core 5 characters emerge
Use AI-controlled battles for speed on weaker encounters

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating random generals and recruiting them
Resource abundance (money and food plentiful in later game)
typical_tension_triggers: Random encounters with unique enemy generals
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradually escalating from village defense to continental unification; early game features grinding and recruitment loops; mid-to-late game transitions to strategic faction wars with diminishing exploration novelty

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible introductory combat against basic Yellow Turban forces; player builds militia from peasants and farmers
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalates with recruitment of varied generals; multi-faction pressures emerge; increasing party composition complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Becomes challenging without being unfair; core 5 main characters dominate; exploration grows monotonous; excess resources and unusable generals reduce friction
notable_spikes: First major general encounters requiring tactical recruitment strategy
Multi-front invasions from Cao Wei and Sun Wu factions
Mid-to-late game difficulty plateau where core team power overshadows roster depth

notable_level_patterns.description: Unlike typical RPGs, random battles feature named enemy generals specific to the current region rather than generic units. Defeated generals may be recruited for cost, removing them from future encounters. Creates persistent world state.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Various regional zones during early-to-mid game

notable_level_patterns.description: Player advances through regions as Liu Bei expands from village militia to provincial governor to continental unifier, reflecting Romance of the Three Kingdoms narrative structure with branching paths
Late game features coordinated invasions from rival kingdoms (Sun Wu, Cao Wei), escalating from regional skirmishes to continental scale conflicts
